Interface EnvInput

All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractEnvInput, EmptyEnvInput, KeYFile, KeYUserProblemFile, LDTInput, SLEnvInput
@NullMarked public interface EnvInput
Represents an entity read to produce an environment to read a proof obligation. Environment means the initial configuration of a prover containing namespaces and Java model.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Path
    getInitialFile()
    Returns the initial Path which is loaded if available.
    default @Nullable Path
    getJavaFile()
    Returns the file path to specific requested Java file.
    int
    getNumberOfChars()
    Returns the total numbers of chars that can be read in this input.
    Profile
    getProfile()
    Returns the Profile to use.
    default boolean
    isIgnoreOtherJavaFiles()
    This flag determines whether the given path to the Java source should be considered as a classpath, or just the Java file without other files should be loaded.
    String
    name()
    Returns the name of this input.
    ImmutableSet<PositionedString>
    read()
    Reads the input using the given modification strategy, i.e., parts of the input do not modify the initial configuration while others do.
    @Nullable Path
    readBootClassPath()
    gets the boot classpath element, null if none set.
    List<Path>
    readClassPath()
    gets the classpath elements to be considered here.
    Includes
    readIncludes()
    Reads the include section and returns an Includes object.
    @Nullable Path
    readJavaPath()
    Reads the Java path.
    void
    setInitConfig(InitConfig initConfig)
    Sets the initial configuration the read environment input should be added to.

  • Method Details

    • name

      String name()
      Returns the name of this input.

    • getNumberOfChars

      int getNumberOfChars()
      Returns the total numbers of chars that can be read in this input.

    • setInitConfig

      void setInitConfig(InitConfig initConfig)
      Sets the initial configuration the read environment input should be added to. Must be called before calling any of the read* methods.

    • readIncludes

      Includes readIncludes() throws ProofInputException
      Reads the include section and returns an Includes object.
      Throws:
      ProofInputException

    • readJavaPath

      @Nullable Path readJavaPath() throws ProofInputException
      Reads the Java path.
      Throws:
      ProofInputException

    • getJavaFile

      default @Nullable Path getJavaFile() throws ProofInputException
      Returns the file path to specific requested Java file.
      Throws:
      ProofInputException
      See Also:
      • isIgnoreOtherJavaFiles()

    • readClassPath

      List<Path> readClassPath() throws ProofInputException
      gets the classpath elements to be considered here.
      Throws:
      ProofInputException

    • readBootClassPath

      @Nullable Path readBootClassPath()
      gets the boot classpath element, null if none set.

    • read

      ImmutableSet<PositionedString> read() throws ProofInputException
      Reads the input using the given modification strategy, i.e., parts of the input do not modify the initial configuration while others do.
      Returns:
      The found warnings or an empty ImmutableSet if no warnings occurred.
      Throws:
      ProofInputException

    • getProfile

      Profile getProfile()
      Returns the Profile to use.
      Returns:
      The Profile to use.

    • getInitialFile

      Path getInitialFile()
      Returns the initial Path which is loaded if available.
      Returns:
      The initial Path which is loaded or null otherwise.

    • isIgnoreOtherJavaFiles

      default boolean isIgnoreOtherJavaFiles()
      This flag determines whether the given path to the Java source should be considered as a classpath, or just the Java file without other files should be loaded.

      Default is false.

      If true, the requested Java file has to given via getJavaFile().

      For further information see the readJava(EnvInput, InitConfig) method of class ProblemInitializer
      See Also:
      • ProblemInitializer
